notes: Two-rule fixture: (1) TQ-1 mid-sentence juga between subject and verb ('dia juga datang' = 'they also came'); (2) TQ-3 gender-neutral 'dia → they'. Expected_target 'They came too' (rather than 'They also came') keeps the rule's sentence-final inject idempotent — when NMT preserves the marker, substitution happens at the marker's position; when NMT drops it, the inject is sentence-final. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'They came too' is the safer rendering for this rule; 'They also came' would be more natural but mid-sentence inject is harder.
